summary: Set 098 Session 1: shipped the optional `kind: plan | decomposition` spec-config contract (module-lifecycle verdict decision 5). `SessionSetKind` type + `SessionSetConfig.kind` (raw, as-authored) and `SessionSet.kind` (validated) in src/types.ts; raw capture in parseSessionSetConfig plus warn-and-degrade validation in readSessionSets (fileSystem.ts) — an unknown value warns and reads as an ordinary work set, never a refusal; absent stays undefined so every pre-098 spec is valid unchanged. Documented in docs/spec-md-schema.md (field reference + semantics paragraph) and the authoring guide (config-block snippet, Field semantics entry with the scaffolder-output-only rule, Defaults note); the spec template snippet deliberately gains no kind line. Python mirror record-and-skipped per spec step 3: every ai_router config-block reader key-plucks its own fields, so kind is inert there and no consumer exists yet (activity-log step 3). 11 new Layer 2 tests (sessionSetKind.test.ts: parse matrix absent/plan/decomposition/unknown/malformed + readSessionSets round-trip, warn-count, case-tolerance). Suites: pytest 3030 passed/6 skipped; extension unit 1522/1522; Playwright Layer 3 run locally per L-064-12 with one pre-existing-surface palette-timing flake that passed 4/4 on isolated re-run; tsc + esbuild clean. Cross-provider phased discovery (K=2, gpt-5-6) returned VERIFIED with zero findings on round 1. No version bump: single release boundary after Set 101.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (s1-next-set-analysis.json, gemini-pro): Session 2 is deterministic implementation of a settled design (two spec templates + the guarded scaffoldModuleLifecycleSets writer + tests), so the cheapest-capable anthropic model suffices and keeps the openai/google verification pool free. Runner-up: claude-sonnet-5.
